name: MCV_full_genome
description: MCV complete circular dsDNA genome ~5387 bp. Tropism / disease: Merkel-cell carcinoma (MCC); virus integrates with truncating LT mutations that retain RB-binding but lose helicase activity. Genome organization: early region (large T, small t, and in JCV/MCV additional splice paralogs) on one strand; late region (VP1, VP2, VP3, and in some polyomaviruses agnoprotein) on the opposite strand; non-coding control region (NCCR / regulatory region) between them containing the bidirectional promoter and the origin of replication. Replicates via host DNA polymerase recruited by large-T at ori. MCV is unique among human polyomaviruses in being directly oncogenic: in MCC tumors, MCV integrates and accumulates truncating mutations in LT that abolish helicase/origin-unwinding activity while preserving the RB-binding LxCxE motif.
expected_copy_count: thousands of episomal copies per productive cell; in MCC, 1-15 clonally-integrated copies per tumor
